history.content:
<thought>
I am facing forward with the agent at the bottom center. The path ahead shows a wall directly in front, so moving forward is not possible. The lava (L) cells are mostly to the right side, and walls (#) form a vertical barrier ahead. I cannot move forward, so I will consider turning. Turning right or left might open a path. Turning right will face me to the left side of the map where I see floor cells (.). I will turn right to check if I can move safely in that direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me (above my position) is lava (L), which is deadly and must be avoided. To find a safe path, I need to turn away from the lava. Turning right will face me towards a wall (#), which is not traversable. Turning left will open a path to empty floor (.) cells, which are safe to move into. Therefore, I will turn left to try to avoid the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see a wall column in front of me extending upwards. To the right, the cells are free floor space, while to the left and forward there is mostly wall and dangerous lava cells. Moving forward is blocked by the wall. I should turn right to explore the free space and potentially find a path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom center and facing forward. The front cell has a wall (#) which means I cannot move forward. To find an alternative path, I need to turn either left or right. To the right, there is a wall as well (#), so turning right would not be helpful. To the left, there are lava cells (L) but I don't know what's beyond them due to the unknown cells (?). It's risky to move directly into lava, but turning left will allow me to turn and potentially find a way around the wall. So I will turn left first to explore.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<action>

<action>
